heroHeadline: $6.6 Trillion Two-Day Market Crash; China Imposes 34% Tariff on All US Goods; Liberation Day Anniversary Expands Metal & Pharma Tariffs
heroSubtitle: The largest two-day stock market loss in history — $6.6 trillion — unfolded April 3–4 after Trump expanded metal tariffs (50%) and announced 100% pharmaceutical tariffs on Liberation Day's one-year anniversary (Apr 2), followed by China's 34% retaliatory tariff on all US imports effective April 10. S&P 500 is down 10% from peak; Nasdaq in bear market. Markets partially stabilized April 6 on the Muscat Protocol (Iran-Oman Strait of Hormuz shipping agreement) after oil peaked at $113/barrel. March jobs report (BLS Apr 3) showed 178K added but February was revised to a net loss of 92K — first payroll decline since 2020. JPMorgan recession probability at 60%; Goldman Sachs at 35%. Fed constrained by core PCE at 3.06% from cutting ahead of April 29–30 FOMC. China tariffs take effect April 10.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Conference Board, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Yale Budget Lab, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy
